methods.description: Process for calculating average climate data values and preparing them for analysis in the study. This involves adjusting the resolution of the data and calculating averages over specified time periods.

steps.description: Calculate average values of temperature and rainfall over the whole baseline time period in a 0.5 degree resolution grid consistent with the yield dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average temperature and rainfall values over baseline period at 0.5 degree resolution

steps.description: Calculate yearly averages for the study period between 8,050 BCE to 550 BCE.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate yearly averages of climate data for specified study period

steps.description: Perform linear interpolation on atmospheric CO2 concentrations to get a yearly time series.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate CO2 concentration data to create yearly time series

methods.description: Investigation of the effects of different model variants on simulation outcomes. This involves modifying model parameters and observing the resulting behavior.

steps.description: Employ the logistic model of group fission instead of a linear dependence on population.
steps.searchable_summary: implement logistic model for group fission probability

steps.description: Consider an alternate case where migration still happens even if the target cell is occupied, and newly created aggressors take over the target cell.
steps.searchable_summary: modify migration model to allow aggressors to take over occupied cells

steps.description: Modify model so that aggressors do not consider the population of potential target cells.
steps.searchable_summary: remove population consideration from aggressor target selection
